what_it_does: Stores a sent noticeboard message item that records informal user feedback and a UX request in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: Message authored by tim in channel dragnet-development with state sent
Created and updated timestamps are identical
History contains a single 'filed' transition by tim
No thread or links are attached
Body reports a visible symbol that does not respond to taps and a failure to update live
Author asks for a blanket rule that all requested in-app updates appear live and animate in
standouts: The demand for a universal 'blanket law' requiring live, animated updates for every future in-app change
A visible symbol is reported as non-functional despite being present
The author notes a stale UI that only refreshes after leaving and returning to the screen
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, created, updated, and history
Markdown body follows the frontmatter delimiter
Item filename uses an id hash suffix
concerns: Title is truncated mid-sentence, making the item hard to identify without opening it
No links or thread connect the feedback to a specific feature, issue, or code asset
Language is absolute ('ever ever'), which may complicate scoping and implementation
No structured fields for severity, affected feature, or action items
notes: A feedback noticeboard item reporting a non-responsive symbol and demanding that every in-app update render live with animation.
questions: Which specific symbol or screen is being referenced?
What application/platform is the user interacting with?
What update mechanism currently fails to push changes live?
Are animation expectations intended for all surfaces or only specific ones?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose as a message/feedback record matches its content; it stores a single piece of user feedback.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a hard UX quality rule—every requested in-app update should appear immediately and with animation—while also flagging a specific bug where a visible control does not respond to input.
novelty: The feedback is framed as a universal policy or 'blanket law' rather than a single bug report, extending the requirement to all future updates.
contribution: Captures a concrete UX requirement and a bug symptom (non-responsive symbol, stale UI) that a fused system should retain as a design constraint and work item.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard message by tim in dragnet-development reporting a non-responsive symbol and requesting a blanket rule that all in-app updates render live with animations.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate a UX policy expectation and a specific interaction bug to the development channel.
